revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Funazukayama Kofun]][[ja:舟塚山古墳]]
{{Short description|{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Funatsukayama-kofun kouenbu.JPG|coordinates={{Coord|36.167844|N|140.289722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Funazukayama Kofun | native_name = 舟塚山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Funatsukayama-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Funazukayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Ibaraki Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Funazukayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 10 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|10|3.83|N|140|17|22.66|E|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ishioka, Ibaraki|en|Ishioka, Ibaraki|qid=Q819658|lt=Ishioka, Ibaraki|1=Ishioka, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = ''[[kofun]]'' | part_of = | length = 186 meters | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =mid 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1963, 1972 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (public park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Funazukayama Kofun'''|舟塚山古墳|}} is a large [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located in the Kitanemoto neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Ishioka, Ibaraki|en|Ishioka, Ibaraki|qid=Q819658|lt=Ishioka, Ibaraki|1=Ishioka, Ibaraki}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/215939|title=舟塚山古墳|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 July 2020}}</ref> It is the largest in Ibaraki Prefecture and the second largest in the Kantō region after the [[Ōta Tenjinyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Ōta, Gunma|en|Ōta, Gunma|qid=Q386179|lt=Ōta, Gunma|1=Ōta, Gunma}}.
==Overview== The Funazukayama ''Kofun'' is located on the edge of a plateau on the northern coast of {{ill|Lake Kasumigaura|en|Lake Kasumigaura|qid=Q1077984|lt=Lake Kasumigaura|1=Lake Kasumigaura}}. It has a total length of 186 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, with rows of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', but neither figurative ''haniwa'' nor ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been discovered. It was surrounded by a shield-shaped moat on all sides except the south, which give the tumulus a total length of 260 meters if the moats are considered as part of the length. It is part of a cluster of smaller tumuli in area, and is estimated to have been built around the latter half of the 5th century, or the middleof the Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> As the surrounding area is also the location of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] of {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}} during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, it is speculated that this ''kofun'' may have been the grave of the local ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' or local king during the earlier Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus has undergone some changes in its configuration due to the encroachment of a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} on its grounds, and the mining of the tumulus for soil by local farmers; and it is possible that it was robbed in premodern times, as local legends state that many swords have been uncovered in the area. The tumulus was surveyed by {{ill|Meiji University|en|Meiji University|qid=Q46492|lt=Meiji University|1=Meiji University}} in 1963. In 1972, an [[archaeological excavation]] found a number of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including a straight iron sword and shield, but no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered. It was re-examined from 2011 to 2013 with {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|qid=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}}. The tumulus is located a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Takahama Station (Ibaraki)|en|Takahama Station (Ibaraki)|qid=Q5365228|lt=Takahama Station|1=Takahama Station (Ibaraki)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|qid=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 186 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 99 meters wide x 10 meters high ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 11 meters high
<gallery> File:Funatsukayama Kofun, kouenbu-1.jpg|Overview File:Funatsukayama Kofun, kouenbu-2.jpg|For anterior towards posterior File:Funatsukayama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|From posterior towards anterior File:Funatsukayama-kofun kouenbu.JPG|Posterior circula portion </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|qid=Q16248240|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ibaraki)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:dd]]{{template-inline|Funatsukayama Kofun}} * [https://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/kuni/shiseki/12-4/12-4.html Ibaraki Prefecture Board of Education site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.ishioka.lg.jp/page/page001404.html Ishioka City official site] {{in lang|ja}} Funazukayama Kofun<
{{Kofun navbar}}
